step_by_step_thinking:
Radiation sensitivity varies among tissues, with some being more radiosensitive than others. Bone has significant blood supply and repair mechanisms which help it withstand higher levels of radiation compared to other listed organs or tissues that have fewer reparative capacities after damage from ionizing radiation.

My Answer: A: Bone Your analysis correctly identifies bone as less sensitive to radiation due to its robust healing capacity and good blood supply.
